header.location: Relocating to Portland, Oregon in May 2026

summary: I’m Jamie—a curious, people-loving (97% extrovert!) Talent Development professional who thrives on designing training programs grounded in evidence, analyzing data to improve the workplace, and partnering with stakeholders for sustainable impact. Expect me to always be seeking ways we can strengthen employee readiness—through human-centered, systematic approaches.

bullets.text: Facilitate training sessions for 600+ participants, enhancing program understanding and driving a 78% program enrollment rate
Conduct {data|needs assessments} with 25+ global stakeholders to identify {data|training gaps} and design 3 new educational programs
Develop guides and workflows for 70+ {cross|cross-functional staff}, improving {data|staff knowledge} and reducing {prog|onboarding} time by 75%
Collaborate with {cross|cross-functional teams} to launch 15+ educational programs, refining delivery strategy and operations via feedback
Led a manager effectiveness initiative, transforming feedback data into actionable reports that informed leadership strategies
Analyzed 2,000+ employee responses, communicating insights to C-suite stakeholders to guide decisions on {coord|training} priorities
Provided consultations to improve strategic plans and internal {coord|training} on {ex|DEI}, Inclusive Leadership, and performance review
bullets.change: Swapped in from content library — more relevant to training design for MESD role

bullets.text: Improved employee experience via 5 {cross|cross-functional} projects focusing on survey development, rewards & recognition, and L&D
Coordinated with external vendors and {prog|OSHA} team to deliver employee {coord|workplace safety training}, ensuring compliance and safety
bullets.change: NEW — replaces M&A bullet. Based on Jamie actual experience coordinating safety training with vendors and OSHA team at Vestas

bullets.text: Streamlined {prog|onboarding} across 3 global locations by coordinating process analysis and clarifying job responsibilities
Initiated a {ex|DEI} pilot with an {coord|Inclusive Leadership workshop} for 12 senior leaders, later expanded to 23 other global locations
Strengthened engagement via an HR newsletter and award-winning well-being initiatives, recognized among 1,000+ companies
Designed and {coord|facilitated} a 3-week immersion {prog|curriculum} for 12 students, applying {data|adult learning principles} to improve outcomes

bullets.text: Delivered culturally responsive lessons, adjusting content and pacing in real time to meet diverse {ex|learner needs}
Developed {data|instructional materials} aligned to learning objectives to support knowledge retention and skill application

jd.meta: MESD — Employee Safety & Readiness Training Coordinator (Portland, OR). Cap-exempt public education. Posted Mar 31, 2026.

sections.title: Training Design & Delivery
items: Safety training: crisis prevention, de-escalation, PPE, injury prevention
Apply adult learning principles and best practices
Assess organizational and program-level training needs
Develop safety orientation for new employees

sections.title: Program Coordination & Systems
items: Design and manage training schedules, session logistics, delivery timelines
Oversee safety orientation for substitutes and temporary staff
Track training completion and recertification requirements

sections.title: Injury Prevention & Analysis
items: Review injury and near-miss data to identify patterns
Evaluate training effectiveness and recommend improvements
Handle employee training records with discretion and confidentiality

sections.title: Collaboration & Compliance
items: Partner with HR, Risk Management, and program leaders
